text:
Mengjie Zou
Mengjie Zou serves as the University Librarian at Duke Kunshan University (DKU), where she leads the library’s strategic planning and operations. She works closely with Duke University Libraries to provide high-quality user services, comprehensive support for teaching and research, and multi-format collections that align with academic and research priorities. She also responds to emerging interdisciplinary and technological needs, preserves the university’s history and regional heritage materials, and promotes open access and scholarly communication.
Mengjie joined DKU in 2014 as its first library staff member, playing a key role in the library’s establishment and early development. She later served as Research and Instruction Librarian, leading and coordinating information literacy instruction, supporting research teams across campus in conducting evidence-based reviews, and serving as a library liaison for multiple academic programs. In early 2025, she was appointed Interim University Librarian before formally assuming the role. Her research interests include copyright literacy, evidence synthesis, and AI-driven research methodologies. She is also an active member of several international professional library associations.
Prior to joining DKU, Mengjie served at the University of Pittsburgh Library and Duke University Libraries. She holds a Master’s degree in Library and Information Sciences and a Certificate in Advanced Asian Studies from the University of Pittsburgh, as well as a Bachelor’s degree in Information Management and Information Systems from South China Normal University.
